title: fraxinus bungeana var. obovata
intro: Fraxinus bungeana var. obovata, a variety of the Manchurian ash tree, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its obovate-shaped leaves and ash tree-like properties. The bark and leaves of Fraxinus bungeana var. obovata have been traditionally used in medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of this plant have shown to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and cough. Fraxinus bungeana var. obovata is commonly prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions for its medicinal uses.
